input.issue_title: [Python] Multi-thread support for python asyncio gRPC clients
input.issue_body:
This issue was created at the request of @lidizheng on [a stackoverflow post](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/65945944/multi-thread-support-for-python-asyncio-grpc-clients).

### What version of gRPC and what language are you using?

* Python version: `3.8.6`
* grpcio version: `1.35.0`

### What operating system (Linux, Windows,...) and version?

Issue observed on multiple macOS versions as well as within Ubuntu based docker containers. We have been testing with the `python:3.8.6-slim-buster` image.

### What runtime / compiler are you using (e.g. python version or version of gcc)

Python version: `3.8.6`

### What did you do?

1. Create a gRPC server and launch it
2. Create multiple (two is enough) workers on separate threads
3. Have each thread make calls to the server simultaneously

### What did you expect to see?

No issies.

### What did you see instead?

A stream of the following errors:

```
2021-01-27 09:33:56,937 ERROR [asyncio] [thread_0] Exception in callback PollerCompletionQueue._handle_events()()
handle: )()>
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.8/asyncio/events.py", line 81, in _run
    self._context.run(self._callback, *self._args)
  File "src/python/grpcio/grpc/_cython/_cygrpc/aio/completion_queue.pyx.pxi", line 147, in grpc._cython.cygrpc.PollerCompletionQueue._handle_events
BlockingIOError: [Errno 11] Resource temporarily unavailable
2021-01-27 09:33:56,937 ERROR [asyncio] [thread_1] Exception in callback PollerCompletionQueue._handle_events()()
handle: )()>
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.8/asyncio/events.py", line 81, in _run
    self._context.run(self._callback, *self._args)
  File "src/python/grpcio/grpc/_cython/_cygrpc/aio/completion_queue.pyx.pxi", line 147, in grpc._cython.cygrpc.PollerCompletionQueue._handle_events
BlockingIOError: [Errno 11] Resource temporarily unavailable
``` 

### Anything else we should know about your pro

ground_truth.diff:
commit afe5b7bf52f08c7f6d7fc4f966168d22c6dee26d
Author: Ashesh Vidyut <asheshvidyut@google.com>
Date:   Mon Feb 16 22:31:19 2026 -0800

    [Python] Fix multi-thread exception for Asyncio gRPC clients - Fixes #25364 (#41483)
    
    ### Description
    
    Fix multi-thread exception for python asyncio gRPC clients
    
    Fixes for #25364
    
    Fixes https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thundering_herd_problem
    
    Reasons -
    
    1. `PollerCompletionQueue` is created on line https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/src/python/grpcio/grpc/_cython/_cygrpc/aio/grpc_aio.pyx.pxi#L51 which is a single instance and global.
    2. It creates a single socket - https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/src/python/grpcio/grpc/_cython/_cygrpc/aio/completion_queue.pyx.pxi#L80
    3. When multiple event loops register on the same socket - https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/src/python/grpcio/grpc/_cython/_cygrpc/aio/grpc_aio.pyx.pxi#L89
    **In asyncio, an event loop belongs to a specific thread.** From documentation - https://docs.python.org/3/library/asyncio-eventloop.html - asyncio.get_running_loop()
    Return the running event loop in the current OS thread.
    4. We get a event from C Core, https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/src/python/grpcio/grpc/_cython/_cygrpc/aio/completion_queue.pyx.pxi#L102-L101
    5. Since `_has_fd_monitoring` is set to true for most event loops - including linux platform.
    6. We have Event Loop Bound to read from read socket. https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/src/python/grpcio/grpc/_cython/_cygrpc/aio/completion_queue.pyx.pxi#L60
    7.  When the event is received by the C Core in the step 4, cython puts one byte in the write socket so that all the threads having event loop wakes up and processes the data. https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/src/python/grpcio/grpc/_cython/_cygrpc/aio/completion_queue.pyx.pxi#L116
    https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/src/python/grpcio/grpc/_cython/_cygrpc/aio/completion_queue.pyx.pxi#L154
    8. Thread A wins the race, reads the byte, and handles the event.
    9. Thread B lose the race. By the time they call recv(1), the socket is empty.
    10. Since the socket is non-blocking, recv() raises BlockingIOError (errno 11, EAGAIN/EWOULDBLOCK).
    
    Repro test code
    
    ```py
    import asyncio
    import threading
    import grpc
    from grpc.experimental import aio
    from concurrent import futures
    
    class GenericService:
        @staticmethod
        def UnaryCall(request, context):
            return request
    
    def serve_sync(port):
        server = grpc.server(futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=10))
    
        rpc_method_handlers = {
            'UnaryCall': grpc.unary_unary_rpc_method_handler(
                GenericService.UnaryCall,
            ),
        }
        generic_handler = grpc.method_handlers_generic_handler(
            'grpc.testing.TestService', rpc_method_handlers)
        server.add_generic_rpc_handlers((generic_handler,))
        server.add_insecure_port(f'[::]:{port}')
        server.start()
        return server
    
    async def run_client(port):
        async with aio.insecure_channel(f'localhost:{port}') as channel:
            unary_call = channel.unary_unary('/grpc.testing.TestService/UnaryCall')
            response = await unary_call(b'request')
            assert response == b'request'
    
    def thread_target(port):
        loop = asyncio.new_event_loop()
        asyncio.set_event_loop(loop)
        try:
            loop.run_until_complete(run_client(port))
        finally:
            loop.close()
    
    def main():
        port = 50051
        server = serve_sync(port)
    
        threads = []
        for _ in range(10):
            t = threading.Thread(target=thread_target, args=(port,))
            t.start()
            threads.append(t)
    
        for t in threads:
            t.join()
    
        server.stop(0)
        print("Success")
    
    if __name__ == '__main__':
        main()
    
    ```
    
    ### Testing
    
    Tests added for `CI`.
    
    Closes #41483
    
    COPYBARA_INTEGRATE_REVIEW=https://github.com/grpc/grpc/pull/41483 from asheshvidyut:fix/issue/25364 7f96d6d467b03c0e0bf55399344c87816ff9e236
    PiperOrigin-RevId: 871121618

diff --git a/src/python/grpcio/grpc/_cython/_cygrpc/aio/completion_queue.pyx.pxi b/src/python/grpcio/grpc/_cython/_cygrpc/aio/completion_queue.pyx.pxi
index 24cc2ccd..a6a2472d 100644
--- a/src/python/grpcio/grpc/_cython/_cygrpc/aio/completion_queue.pyx.pxi
+++ b/src/python/grpcio/grpc/_cython/_cygrpc/aio/completion_queue.pyx.pxi
@@ -143,11 +143,17 @@ cdef class PollerCompletionQueue(BaseCompletionQueue):
 
     def _handle_events(self, object context_loop):
         cdef bytes data
         if _has_fd_monitoring:
             # If fd monitoring is working, clean the socket without blocking.
-            data = self._read_socket.recv(1)
+            try:
+                # In case of multiple loops, the read socket might be read by multiple threads.
+                # But only one of them will read the 1 byte sent by the poller thread.
+                # So, we need to handle the case where the socket is already empty.
+                data = self._read_socket.recv(1)
+            except BlockingIOError:
+                pass
         cdef grpc_event event
         cdef CallbackContext *context
 
         while True:
             self._queue_mutex.lock()
diff --git a/src/python/grpcio_tests/tests_aio/tests.json b/src/python/grpcio_tests/tests_aio/tests.json
index 32789b12..5c9850ed 100644
--- a/src/python/grpcio_tests/tests_aio/tests.json
+++ b/src/python/grpcio_tests/tests_aio/tests.json
@@ -8,10 +8,11 @@
   "tests_aio.status.grpc_status_test.StatusTest",
   "tests_aio.unit._metadata_test.TestMetadataWithServer",
   "tests_aio.unit._metadata_test.TestTypeMetadata",
   "tests_aio.unit.abort_test.TestAbort",
   "tests_aio.unit.aio_rpc_error_test.TestAioRpcError",
+  "tests_aio

input.issue_body:
Currently we're using [2019.11.27 version](https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/tools/distrib/check_pytype.sh#L17), we want to update it to match the version used in g3.

Current version of pytype also have lots of `pyi-error` which we have to manually disable, ideally we should be able to remove those `pytype: disable=pyi-error` lines too after update.

ground_truth.diff:

    ### Description
    
    Fixes issue #31706, removes `pytype: disable=pyi-error`
    
    ### Testing
    
    CI
    
    Closes #41480

input.pr_title: [export] Add new API to set the key exchange group.
input.pr_body:
[export] Add new API to set the key exchange group.

This PR adds an API through TlsCredentialsOptions to optionally set a Key Exchange group for the underlying SSL implementation. It also an API for the associated enums for Key Exchange groups.

No default behavior should change, this is purely opt-in.

----
DO NOT SUBMIT. This PR is for testing purposes only. [cl/884582493](http://cl/884582493)
